channel.title: About on Nicholas Sizemore
channel.description: Recent content in About on Nicholas Sizemore

item.title: Microbiome dynamics
item.description: 1 Dynamics of the Microbiome 1 Dynamics of the Microbiome Note: This page represents a work-in-progress. More details still to come. However, some slides for a recent talk discussing this model are available here.

item.title: HLB Model
item.description: 1 Multi-scale Stochastic Spread of HLB 2 Overview of Model 2.1 Spatial structure 2.2 Dispersal mechanisms 2.2.1 Local Psyllid Movement 2.2.2 Sales of Plants 2.2.3 Truck Dispersal 1 Multi-scale Stochastic Spread of HLB The spread of huanglongbing (黃龍病) through the state of Florida was driven through a variety of (stochastic) processes whose influence was exerted on different spatial and temporal scales. In generating a cohesive model capable of faithfully explicating spread over large regions, we have to confront the challenge of modeling on these different scales simultaneously.

item.title: About
item.description: About Me This is a Hugo based resume template. You can find the full source code on GitHub. Research Interest Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. item.title: Contact
item.description: Email: ncsizemore@uchicago.edu Address Knapp Center for Biomedical Discovery 900 East 57th St., KCBD #10128 Chicago, IL 60637

item.title: Projects
item.description: Current Projects Inferring dynamics in microbial communities From a small number of longitudinal microbiome profiles in pre-term infants, used a novel machine learning approach to infer the complex time-varying ecoystem dependencies present. From the inferred structure, generated accurate forecasts of microbial evolution, identified infants at risk of poor cognitive development, and found interpretable, testable hypotheses for the key relationships driving network behavior. Stochastic spread of HLB in Florida Analyzed and explicated the historical spread of citrus greening disease in Florida via stochastic dispersal mechanisms of varied temporal and spatial scales.

item.title: Research
item.description: Research Interests Applied mathematician focused on the modeling and understanding of high-dimensional complex systems by integrating modern tools from the theory of stochastic processes, machine learning, and mathematical data science. This includes work on interdisciplinary applications and theoretical problems explored from a computational perspective. Current/ongoing projects are listed below; see the Projects page for more details. Current Projects – Inferring dynamics of the microbiome – Algorithmic detection of deceptive/adversial responses to surveys

item.title: Teaching
item.description: While I am not currently teaching any courses, I have several years of experience as an instructor through appointments at the following institutions. University of Florida As a graduate student at UF, I taught classes as the primary instructor (responsible for all course content), and as a teaching assistant. Specific courses include: MAC 1140: Precalculus Algebra MAC 1147: Precalculus Algebra & Trigonometry MAC 2311: Calculus I MAC 2312: Calculus II MAC 2313: Calculus III Western Carolina University
